description: The law of dependent origination — the central causal framework of Buddha Dhamma. Describes how suffering arises and ceases through a twelve-fold chain of conditioned links (nidāna): avijjā → saṅkhāra → viññāṇa → nāmarūpa → saḷāyatana → phassa → vedanā → taṇhā → upādāna → bhava → jāti → jarāmaraṇa. The reverse sequence — cessation of avijjā leads to cessation of the entire chain — is the path to Nibbāna. Paticca samuppāda is inseparable from the tilakkhaṇa and the Four Noble Truths.
